turns.thought: [OBSERVE] At iteration 419, the swarm shows extreme premature convergence. Normalized diversity is 1.84e-16, position std mean 1.21e-15, swarm radius 2.42e-14. All velocities are zero (velocity_zero_ratio = 1.0). Fitness best is 19.8998, and it has not improved for 200 iterations. The fitness mean, worst, and best are essentially identical (std 6.86e-15). The swarm has collapsed to a single point in the search space, far from the global optimum (0). | [DIAGNOSE] The swarm has completely converged to a local minimum of the highly multimodal Rastrigin function. The collapse of diversity and zero velocities indicate that the particles are no longer exploring. The current fitness value ~19.9 is a poor local optimum, likely an attractor far from the global minimum. The root cause is that the swarm lost exploration capability too early, leaving it permanently stuck. | [STRATEGIZE] A strong diversification intervention is needed to escape this local basin. Since all particles are essentially identical, resetting a substantial fraction of the worst particles (which are all practically the same, so any subset) to random positions with fresh velocities will inject diversity. This is better than levy_flight or gaussian_mutation because the convergence is absolute and those methods perturb around the current best, which may not be sufficient to jump out of a deep basin. Opposition_reinit is not suitable as particles are not at boundaries. I will reset 50% of particles, re-initialize velocities, and set inertia weight to 1.1 to promote continued exploration.
turns.observation:

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The reset_worst_particles action successfully reinitialized 15 particles (50% of swarm), which should have injected substantial diversity. However, the global best fitness remained at 19.8998, as expected because we did not touch the best particle. The converged elite particles still dominate the swarm's attractor, pulling the newly randomized particles toward the same local optimum. Diagnosis was correct: the swarm was collapsed, but just adding random particles may not be enough to escape a deep local basin if the best particle stays put. The exploration may take many iterations, and the best itself might never move unless directly perturbed. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The local optimum is a strong attractor. Although diversity has been partially restored by new random particles, the global best particle still holds the swarm in the basin. To accelerate escape, we need to also relocate the best particle to potentially discover a better basin. Without that, the swarm will likely reconverge to the same 19.9 region.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The reset_worst_particles successfully added 15 new random particles, but the global best remained unchanged at 19.8998, anchoring the swarm. The levy_flight on best particles perturbed two near-best particles, but did not yield an improvement in the global best, suggesting the local basin is deep and wide. The new random particles are now far from the best and may eventually discover a better region, but they risk being pulled back to the current best if social attraction remains strong. Thus, the immediate effect was no improvement, but the diversity has increased temporarily. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The swarm is stuck in a deep local optimum of the Rastrigin function. The best particle is a strong attractor that will quickly gather even newly randomized particles unless their exploration is encouraged. The current parameter settings likely overemphasize social attraction, suppressing the exploratory potential of the reset particles.
